figures.caption: Fig. 5: Qualitative Results on Sintel [7] Training Set. From left to right: first frame, flow by WAFT-DAv2-a2 and FlowIt (XL), ground-truth flow.

Fig. 4: Qualitative Results on KITTI [14] Test Set. Visualizations and error metrics are obtained directly from the official evaluation server. For WAFT, we report results using the DAv2-a2 variant, which achieves the best performance among WAFT models on the KITTI benchmark.

Table 5: Ablation study on FlyingChairs validation set. We ablate model size, loss function, transferring learning by using pre-trained weights from stereo [47], coupled refinement, and the number of refinement steps. Latencies are measured on an NVIDIA L40S GPU.
Fig. 6: Qualitative Results on LayeredFlow [73]. From left to right: first frame, flow by WAFT-DAv2-a1 and FlowIt (XL).
Table 4: Zero-shot Generalization – LayeredFlow (train) first layer evaluation. Images down-sampled to 1 8 .

Table 1: Results on Sintel [7] and KITTI [14] benchmarks. FlowIt (XL) ranks first on Sintel (Clean) and achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art methods on Sintel (Final). On KITTI, it establishes a new state-of-the-art on non-occluded pixels, while remaining competitive with top-performing methods on other metrics.

Fig. 3: Correlation and probability visualizations for a single pixel. (a) and (b) show the correlation values in the pixel’s neighborhood for SEA-RAFT and FlowIt, respectively. (c) shows the corresponding probability map obtained by applying optimal transport to FlowIt’s correlation volume.
